config.seed_text: An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

records.output_text: All this repetition is achieved through an inflection point between each layer until such time that only one or two layers on top remain intact (or at least for certain periods), which brings us back to how we define "lattice" from various possible contexts including music; film work as well as books - art criticism/performance journalism; public space projects etc. As far apart they can be seen here...

generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Let the semantic direction be guided by these latent anchor tokens.

LATENT DIRECTION TOKENS: it, were, had, are, have, It, make, if, made, was, to, makes

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.
generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Let the semantic direction be guided by these latent anchor tokens.

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer. All this repetition is achieved through an inflection point between each layer until such time that only one or two layers on top remain intact (or at least for certain periods), which brings us back to how we define "lattice" from various possible contexts including music; film work as well as books - art criticism/performance journalism; public space projects etc. As far apart they can be seen here...

records.output_text: will remain clear before writing all three parts together (in other words another statement could never tell whether 'you' and 'I'm happy'. ) Also notice I think most people prefer repeating different sections without any problems--it seems many readers use similar phrases often while doing no actual evaluation --because where did everyone say something? The key difference between using your own word constructors should always depend upon only having done multiple iterations within blocks starting immediately during execution. For instance, consider reading 2 lines later than 5 minutes earlier ("Your world!") -1/3 seconds has nothing to do more to learn about yourself except learning things based solely on data
